title: JAP Receipt v0.1
description: Canonical Joulegram Agent Protocol receipt format per Whitepaper §6. Every joule on the Joulegram network must trace to either a receipt matching this schema or a Treasury receipt (see treasury-receipt.schema.json). This schema is the pure JAP receipt format and intentionally carries no Joulegram-app-specific fields (agent_id, rating, critique) — client applications extend this schema with their own additional properties.

receipt_version.description: Schema version identifier. Locked to jap_v0.1 for Phase 0. Future schema revisions will bump this field and the protocol version.

request_id.description: Unique request identifier assigned by the operator at inference time. Must be unique across the entire ledger history.

model_id.description: Canonical provider-reported model identifier (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-20250514, gpt-4o-2024-08-06, gemini-1.5-pro).

model_version.description: Provider-reported model snapshot or version string. For Anthropic, this is the response header model version. For OpenAI, the 'model' field of the response. For self-hosted models, the operator's version tag.

input_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical input bytes. The canonicalization rule is client-specific and must be documented by each JAP client. For Joulegram photos, the rule is: sha256(image_bytes || utf8(persona_prompt)).

output_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical output bytes (the full provider response body, as received by the operator, before any post-processing).

tokens_in.description: Total input tokens consumed, as reported by the provider. Includes system prompt, user message, and any cached tokens.

tokens_out.description: Total output tokens generated, as reported by the provider.

energy_kj.description: Energy equivalent in kilojoules. At L0, computed as ((tokens_in + tokens_out) × alpha_model × beta_provider) / 1000 where alpha_model is the coefficient from Whitepaper §9.1. At L1+, provided by the provider or measured directly.

energy_attestation.description: Energy attestation level per Whitepaper §10. L0 = coefficient-based (current Phase 0 default). L1 = provider-attested via API headers. L2 = calibrated via benchmark consortium. L3 = TEE-attested via hardware enclave. L4 = zero-knowledge proven.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when the inference completed. Must be in UTC with millisecond precision (e.g., 2026-04-08T14:23:11.142Z). The UTC date of this timestamp must match the merkle_epoch field.

operator_sig.description: Ed25519 signature of the canonical receipt body, base64-encoded. The signature is computed over the receipt JSON with all fields EXCEPT operator_sig itself, serialized with sorted keys, no whitespace, and ASCII-only encoding. See scripts/sign_receipt.py for the reference implementation.

provider_sig.description: Optional provider-side signature. Used at energy attestation levels L1+ when the model provider directly attests to the response. Always null at L0. When present, format is provider-specific and documented in the provider's JAP integration spec.

merkle_epoch.description: UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the Merkle tree epoch this receipt belongs to. Must match the UTC date derived from the timestamp field. Used by the daily Merkle root publisher to group receipts into epochs.

judgment_type.description: Optional. The domain of AI judgment this receipt covers. 'aesthetic' = photo/visual critique, 'code_review' = code quality assessment, 'moderation' = content safety/policy filtering, 'general' = unclassified or custom. Joulegram-produced receipts always set this field. Third-party JAP clients may omit it or extend with custom values in Phase 1+.

sha256Hex.description: SHA-256 digest as lowercase hex with sha256: prefix. Exactly 71 characters total (7 for prefix + 64 hex digits).
